spec_match.rationale: Calculator reasons about dictionary TTL vs deploy cadence vs visit frequency — the right axes. But the header preview / output doesn't show the actual Use-As-Dictionary: ttl=… header value as the blurb promises; the page focuses on a recommendation paragraph rather than emitting the literal header syntax.

interactive.rationale: Four preset buttons (CDN, SaaS, Startup, Static), four parameter inputs (ttl slider, cadence slider, visits slider, file-size number), and a results panel with five live computed stats plus a recommendation box.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature index blurb explicitly promises 'generates the exact Use-As-Dictionary: ttl=… header value for your inputs'. The current page renders a recommendation paragraph and stats but the literal header string with the computed ttl is harder to find than the blurb implies — at minimum it isn't a top-level output.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers low-ttl-high-cadence (lots of misses) via the recommendation; missing: ttl floor cases (1 hour vs 1 day), file-size 1 KB (no real savings) and 10000 KB (huge), and the 30-day freshness bar handles only the happy path.

references.rationale: Links chromestatus.com/feature/5159497770647552 which is NOT this feature — the correct id is 6408307086589952. The wrong link breaks design-system rule 3 (every page must include the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link for THIS feature) and breaks the fix-slugs.py self-heal.

design_system.rationale: All colours via CSS variables; preset-btn override sets both background AND color (per rule 6); progress fills use --accent-emerald/blue/rose; no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: CRITICAL: fix the wrong chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: The 'See also' list links https://chromestatus.com/feature/5159497770647552 — that is a different feature. The correct id for Compression Dictionary TTL is 6408307086589952. This breaks design-system rule 3 (every page must include the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link for THIS feature) and breaks the /tmp/fix-slugs.py self-heal which relies on the embedded id to recover folder names. Fix the href immediately.

openQuestions.title: Surface the literal Use-As-Dictionary: ttl=<seconds> header
openQuestions.detail: The feature-index blurb promises 'generates the exact Use-As-Dictionary: ttl=… header value for your inputs'. The page should have a copyable code block that emits the full computed header (with the ttl in seconds, the match/dest pattern, and id) so authors can paste it straight into their server config. Currently the calculator stops at a stats panel.

openQuestions.title: Add a 'compare two TTLs' mode
openQuestions.detail: The calculator currently shows ONE configuration's outcome. The interesting analysis is comparative — e.g. ttl=7d vs ttl=30d for the same deploy cadence — so the user can see why a longer ttl actually hurts the variant explosion case from the sibling concept. Add a second column of inputs and a delta column.

summary: Useful calculator but two blocking issues: the chromestatus link points to the wrong feature id (5159497770647552 instead of 6408307086589952), and the page never emits the literal Use-As-Dictionary: ttl=<seconds> header that the blurb claims it generates. Fix those two first; then add a comparative mode.
